design_decisions.d0_open_oeq_residuals: Open-ended question(s) left as the note's acknowledged open problem for D0.5 review: oeq:causal-dp-frontier

added_assumptions.label: positive-covariate-dimension
added_assumptions.statement: hd : 0 < d — the covariate dimension is positive. Threaded into private_local_polynomial_upper_bound / certified_private_cate_bracket / equal_smoothness_sharp_private_rate. REGULARITY ONLY: rStar r0 x0 = (1/2)·min r0 (⨅ i : Fin d, min (x0 i) (1-x0 i)) uses a ciInf over Fin d, which is a junk value when d = 0; the note's model (covariates X on the cube [0,1]^d with a density bounded below) presupposes d ≥ 1. Does not weaken the claim.

flags.f3_filler_directive:
The regression-inheritance EMBEDDING substrate is BUILT and PROVEN — invoke it, never re-derive it.

Helpers/RegressionEmbedding.lean provides: regToTreated (the measurable regression-datum (x,z) -> treated observation (Y=z,A=1,X=x) map) + regToTreated_replacementAdjacent (coordinatewise adjacency preservation); cateWitnessRegressionLaw Q b (the one-server pointwise-regression law) with _isProbabilityMeasure / _map_fst / _isRegressionFn (E[Z|X] = b); cateWitnessControlLaw Q (the b-FREE shared control law) with _isProbabilityMeasure / _support ({A=0}); and the crux cateWitnessLaw_dataMeasure_mixture: (cateWitnessLaw Q e0 b).dataMeasure = ofReal e0 . ((cateWitnessRegressionLaw Q b).map regToTreated) + ofReal (1-e0) . cateWitnessControlLaw Q.

Helpers/CausalLowerBound.lean provides causal_oracle_private_lower_bound_witness — the SAME lower bound as causal_oracle_private_lower_bound but with the model member Q and the two bump regression functions bnp/bp EXPOSED, so the null and both alternatives are literal 'cateWitnessLaw Q e0 .' applications. Any goal needing the LAW-LEVEL structure of the lower family (mixture identities, identical control arms, the regression reduction) MUST obtain from the _witness form; the plain form hides the construction and those goals are unprovable from it.

PERFORMANCE — never put bare 'cateWitnessLaw' in a simp set: it unfolds the two enormous noncomputable Measure fields into the proof term and the KERNEL dies with a deterministic timeout. Use the @[simp] rfl projection lemmas cateWitnessLaw_mu0_apply / _mu1_apply / _mu1_eq / _pi_apply / _PX_eq / _px_apply instead. Same reason: do not simp-unfold a let-bound CateLaw.
flags.f2_scaffold_directive: POTENTIAL OUTCOMES ARE LATENT — never a function of the observed record. Model the causal law as a joint law of (Y(0), Y(1), A, X): the potential outcomes are random variables on the law's own probability space, and the OBSERVED outcome is DERIVED by consistency Y = Y(A). NEVER type a potential-outcome field as a map out of the OBSERVED record (e.g. `pot : R -> Obs -> R` where Obs already contains Y) — that makes the counterfactual a deterministic function of observed data, contradicts the fundamental problem of causal inference, and silently restricts the class (with discrete outcomes it forces mu_1 = +/- mu_0, making standard one-arm-perturbed two-point constructions unrealizable). Conditional exchangeability must be stated as (Y(0),Y(1)) independent of A given X on that joint law.

proposed_from.topic: Differentially private minimax rate for pointwise CATE estimation under Holder smoothness and central (eps,delta)-DP: the sharp two-sided rate for tau(x0)=E[Y(1)-Y(0)|X=x0] pairing a private higher-order-influence-function doubly-robust R-learner (upper bound, with privatized estimation of the alpha-smooth propensity and beta-smooth outcome nuisances) against a localized fingerprinting/tracing lower bound, extending the non-private CATE minimax rate of Kennedy-Balakrishnan-Robins-Wasserman (2024, arXiv 2203.00837) with a derived DP-cost term and a new privacy-dominated regime/phase boundary in (smoothness, dimension, privacy) space; differs from upper-bound-only private-CATE methods (DP-CATE 2503.03486, Niu 2202.11043) and from private pointwise nonparametric regression (Cai-Chakraborty-Vuursteen 2406.06755) by delivering the matching causal-functional lower bound

banked_reason: F5-clean: all 7 headline results proven and axiom-clean (barrier, converse, achievability, certified two-sided bracket, sharp beta=gamma rate, regression inheritance); zero substrate gates; F4 converged with both reviewers matched. Banked at novelty tier subfield per user decision.
